Four consecutive Edits on playwright.perf.config.ts driven by iterative test-run results (externally justified iteration rather than blind retry). First cycle where Read-before-retry behavior appeared within an edit-thrash-adjacent unit rather than hook-bypass as exit. Not yet confirmed improvement — single-cycle signal.
recommendations: shell_tool_preference_violations and compound_command_overreach are STABLE across 4+ consecutive cycles with identical structural form and no frequency reduction. No new recommendations — prior recommendations unimplemented and no new leverage point identified this cycle.

rm_bash_instead_of_trash: safe-delete hook is catching violations correctly. No additional recommendation beyond what the existing hook provides; root-cause is the same pre-selection gap as the broader shell violations pattern.

re_edit_thrash: Read-before-retry behavior in db668075-21 is a positive signal worth tracking for one more cycle before drawing conclusions. If confirmed in next cycle, this may warrant downgrading re_edit_thrash from RECURRING:STABLE to RECURRING:MARGINAL_IMPROVEMENT.
